overview: A Frappe custom app that integrates ERPNext HRMS with LHDN MyInvois for Malaysian e-Invoice compliance. When a Salary Slip or Expense Claim is submitted, the app evaluates LHDN exemption rules, asynchronously builds a UBL 2.1 XML self-billed e-Invoice (type '11'), submits it to LHDN via HTTP POST (base64+SHA-256 wrapped), writes the UUID/QR/status back to ERPNext, and handles polling, retries, cancellation, and monthly consolidation. Built on top of the installed myinvois_erpgulf app â€” only its taxpayerlogin module is reused; XML building and HTTP submission are implemented independently.
goals: Achieve LHDN e-Invoice compliance for payroll and expense claim self-billing (YA 2026+)
Zero modifications to ERPNext, HRMS, or myinvois_erpgulf source files â€” hooks only
Fully async â€” never block WSGI workers during LHDN API calls
Support both real-time per-document submission and monthly consolidated (code 004) grace-period mode
All 5 LHDN mandatory test scenarios pass in sandbox before production cutover

userStories.title: Implement on_submit hooks with async enqueue
userStories.description: Create lhdn_payroll_integration/services/submission_service.py with enqueue_salary_slip_submission(doc, method) and enqueue_expense_claim_submission(doc, method). Each function: (1) calls the exemption filter and sets custom_lhdn_status='Exempt' then returns if not in scope; (2) validates document name length; (3) calls frappe.enqueue with enqueue_after_commit=True; (4) sets custom_lhdn_status='Pending'. userStories.title: Implement UBL 2.1 XML payload builder for Salary Slip
userStories.description: Create the UBL 2.1 XML builder for self-billed Salary Slip e-Invoices in lhdn_payroll_integration/services/payload_builder.py. The builder uses Python's xml.etree.ElementTree with the exact LHDN UBL namespaces. Self-billed inversion: Employer = Buyer (payer), Contractor = Supplier (payee). e-Invoice type code = '11'. Financial totals MUST be calculated from doc.earnings child table only â€” never from YTD fields or compute_year_to_date() (v16 bug). All monetary values use decimal.Decimal with ROUND_HALF_UP. The function also provides prepare_submission_wrapper() which wraps the XML in the LHDN HTTP submission format (base64 + SHA-256).
acceptanceCriteria: build_salary_slip_xml(docname) returns a valid XML string with root element <Invoice xmlns='urn:oasis:names:specification:ubl:schema:xsd:Invoice-2'>
InvoiceTypeCode element contains '11'
AccountingSupplierParty contains employee TIN and name (payee = supplier)
AccountingCustomerParty contains company TIN and name (payer = buyer)
For foreign worker employee, SupplierTIN = 'EI00000000010'
TotalExcludingTax + TotalTaxAmount == TotalIncludingTax (exact Decimal equality, 2dp)
YTD fields (doc.ytd_net_pay, doc.year_to_date) are never accessed
prepare_submission_wrapper(xml_string, docname) returns dict with 'documents' list containing format, document (base64), documentHash (SHA-256 hex), codeNumber keys

Register namespaces with ET.register_namespace before building
InvoiceTypeCode: <cbc:InvoiceTypeCode listVersionID='1.1'>11</cbc:InvoiceTypeCode>
Calculate totals: total_excl = sum(quantize(Decimal(str(e.amount))) for e in doc.earnings)

userStories.title: Implement async LHDN cancellation job
userStories.description: Add process_cancellation(doctype, docname) to cancellation_service.py. This background worker calls the LHDN PUT /documents/{uuid}/state endpoint with status='cancelled'. On success: set custom_lhdn_status='Cancelled' and cancel the Frappe document programmatically. On LHDN API rejection: keep status 'Valid' and write error to custom_error_log â€” do NOT cancel the Frappe document.
acceptanceCriteria: On LHDN API success: custom_lhdn_status = 'Cancelled'
On LHDN API success: Frappe document is cancelled (docstatus = 2)
On LHDN API error: custom_lhdn_status remains unchanged
On LHDN API error: Frappe document remains submitted (docstatus = 1)
On LHDN API error: custom_error_log contains API error message

technicalNotes: LHDN cancellation: PUT {base_url}/api/v1.0/documents/{uuid}/state

On HTTP error: parse response JSON for error message, write to custom_error_log via frappe.db.set_value

userStories.title: Implement consolidated payload builder (classification code 004)
userStories.description: Add build_consolidated_xml(docnames, target_month) to payload_builder.py. This builds a single UBL 2.1 XML that aggregates multiple Salary Slip or Expense Claim documents into one e-Invoice. Key differences from individual: InvoiceCode = CONSOL-{company}-{YYYY-MM}, BillingPeriod = full target month, one InvoiceLine per source document (not per salary component), classification code on line items = '004', buyer contact can be 'NA'.

BillingPeriodStartDate = first day of target month, EndDate = last day
